common_assertions: Output is a complete HTML file starting with <!DOCTYPE html>
Uses named import: import { App } from ... (not default import: import App from ...)
Uses result.structuredContent to access tool data (not result.data, not result directly)
Defines an escapeHtml function and uses it for user data in innerHTML
Does not use max-width on the main container
Uses var(--color Fluent design tokens for colors instead of hardcoded values

evals.prompt: Create a map widget showing tourist attractions in San Francisco.

attractions.name: Golden Gate Bridge
attractions.description: Iconic suspension bridge spanning the Golden Gate strait

attractions.name: Fisherman's Wharf
attractions.description: Waterfront neighborhood with shops, restaurants, and sea lions

attractions.name: Alcatraz Island
attractions.description: Former federal prison on an island in San Francisco Bay

attractions.name: Golden Gate Park
attractions.description: Large urban park with museums, gardens, and trails

assertions: Uses a mapping library with interactive markers
Loads map library before Fluent UI to avoid global collision
Saves map library global reference to avoid Fluent overwriting it

evals.prompt: Create a weather widget showing current conditions and 5-day forecast.
Create a sales dashboard with KPI cards and a monthly revenue chart.
assertions: Shows key metrics as cards (revenue, target, growth)
Includes a bar or line chart
Formats currency values with $ and commas

evals.prompt: Create a task/ticket list showing sprint tasks with status and priority.
Create a product comparison widget showing headphones side by side.
assertions: Shows products in columns or cards
Displays price, rating, and key specs

evals.prompt: Create an event schedule widget for a tech conference.

events.title: Opening Keynote: The Next Platform Shift
events.speaker: Diane Zhao

assertions: Displays time, title, speaker, and track

evals.prompt: Create a code review summary widget for a pull request.

data.title: Add webhook retry configuration
data.author: John Doe
data.summary: Adds configurable retry logic for webhook deliveries with exponential backoff, max retries, and dead letter queue support.

assertions: Shows PR title, author, and stats

evals.prompt: Create a restaurant menu widget with grouped categories and dietary badges.

items.name: Miso Soup
items.description: Traditional soybean soup with tofu and seaweed

items.name: Chicken Teriyaki
items.description: Grilled chicken thigh with teriyaki glaze, rice, and vegetables

items.name: Tonkotsu Ramen
items.description: Rich pork bone broth, chashu, egg, noodles

evals.prompt: Create a translation widget showing source and target text with alternatives.

data.source_text: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. This sentence contains every letter of the English alphabet.
data.translation: 素早い茶色の狐が怠惰な犬を飛び越える。この文は英語のアルファベットの全ての文字を含んでいます。

alternatives.text: 速い茶色のキツネが怠け者の犬を飛び越える。
茶色い狐がすばしこく怠け犬を飛び越えた。

evals.prompt: Create a dashboard overview widget showing app health metrics and service status.
assertions: Shows KPI cards for key metrics

evals.prompt: Create a stock portfolio widget showing holdings with price and daily change.
assertions: Lists holdings with ticker, price, change %
Colors positive green and negative red using Fluent tokens

evals.prompt: Create a flight status widget showing departure and arrival info.
assertions: Displays departure and arrival side by side

evals.prompt: Create a recipe card widget with ingredients, steps, and nutrition.

data.name: Classic Margherita Pizza
steps: Preheat oven to 500°F with pizza stone inside for 30 minutes.
Crush tomatoes by hand, mix with minced garlic and salt.
Stretch dough into 12-inch round on floured surface.
Spread thin layer of tomato sauce, leaving 1-inch border.
Tear mozzarella into pieces and distribute evenly.
Bake on pizza stone for 10-12 minutes until crust is golden.
Top with fresh basil and drizzle with olive oil. Serve immediately.

assertions: Shows recipe name and time badges

evals.prompt: Create a fitness tracker widget showing steps, calories, heart rate, and weekly trend.
Create a meeting notes widget with attendees, decisions, and action items.

data.title: Q1 Planning Review
data.summary: Reviewed Q1 progress and set priorities for remaining weeks. Agreed to prioritize mobile performance and defer the dashboard redesign.

decisions.decision: Prioritize mobile performance optimization over dashboard redesign
Ship dark mode by end of Q1

action_items.task: Schedule user interviews for new onboarding flow

assertions: Shows action items with assignees and due dates

Create a book recommendations widget with ratings, genres, and descriptions.

recommendations.title: The Obsidian Meridian
recommendations.author: Lena Voss
recommendations.description: A cartographer on a tidally locked planet discovers that the permanent twilight zone is shifting, threatening the only habitable strip of land.

recommendations.title: Hollow Crescents
recommendations.author: Tariq Nassar
recommendations.description: Twin siblings inherit opposing halves of a dying god's power and must cooperate before the last temple collapses.

recommendations.title: Voidloom
recommendations.author: Ingrid Solheim
recommendations.description: A textile engineer aboard a generation ship realizes the fabric of the hull is alive and slowly rewriting the crew's memories.

recommendations.title: Ashfall Covenant
recommendations.author: Davi Rocha
recommendations.description: After a volcanic winter blankets the realm, rival guilds of fire-shapers compete to reignite the sun forge before famine takes hold.

recommendations.title: The Copper Parallax
recommendations.author: Suki Arden
recommendations.description: A retired smuggler is pulled back into service when a rogue satellite network starts broadcasting coordinates to a place that shouldn't exist.

assertions: Shows book cards with title and author

Create a weather alerts widget with severity-colored cards.

alerts.headline: Heavy snow expected above 2000ft
alerts.description: A strong Pacific front will bring 8-14 inches of snow to elevations above 2000ft. Travel over mountain passes will be hazardous.

alerts.headline: Rising rivers may cause minor flooding
alerts.description: Rain combined with snowmelt may cause rivers to rise above flood stage. Low-lying areas near rivers should monitor conditions.

alerts.headline: Gusty winds expected this afternoon
alerts.description: South winds 25-35 mph with gusts to 50 mph expected. Secure loose outdoor objects. Power outages possible.

assertions: Shows alert cards with severity styling

evals.prompt: Create a changelog/release notes widget showing versions with features and fixes.

features: Dark mode support for web dashboard
fixes: Fixed sync conflict resolution on mobile
Resolved memory leak in background sync service
Fixed timezone display in activity log
Corrected file count in sync summary notification
Resolved slow search on large repositories

evals.prompt: Create a quiz results widget showing score, questions, and correct/incorrect answers.

questions.question: What is the output of typeof null?
Which method removes the last element from an array?
What is a closure?
questions.user_answer: A function with access to its outer scope
questions.correct_answer: A function that retains access to its lexical scope

questions.question: What is the default value of an uninitialized variable?
Which is NOT a primitive type?
What does Promise.all() return if one promise rejects?
What is event delegation?
questions.correct_answer: Handling events at a parent level using event bubbling

questions.question: What is the temporal dead zone?
questions.correct_answer: Period between entering scope and let/const declaration

questions.question: What does Array.from() do?
questions.user_answer: Creates an array from an iterable
questions.correct_answer: Creates a new Array from an array-like or iterable object

assertions: Shows user answer vs correct answer for wrong ones

evals.prompt: Create a conference room availability widget showing time slots per room.
Create a podcast episode list widget showing episodes with guests and duration.

episodes.title: The Future of WebAssembly
episodes.description: Maya Chen discusses how WebAssembly is reshaping server-side computing and what WASI means for the future.

episodes.title: Building Accessible-First Design Systems
episodes.description: Practical patterns for making design systems accessible by default rather than as an afterthought.

episodes.title: Edge Computing in Practice
episodes.description: When edge computing makes sense, when it doesn't, and lessons from running code at the edge in production.

episodes.title: Static Typing Deep Dive
episodes.description: A language tooling lead walks through the biggest changes in modern static type systems and the roadmap ahead.

episodes.title: Rethinking State Management
episodes.description: Open-source maintainer Jake Thornton on the evolution of state management and where signals fit in.

assertions: Lists episodes with title, guest, duration

evals.prompt: Create a carbon footprint summary widget showing categories and reduction tips.

tips: Consider carpooling 2 days/week to reduce transportation by 15%
Switch to LED bulbs to save 40kg CO2/year
Try one meatless day per week to reduce food emissions by 10%

evals.prompt: Create a bar chart showing sales rep performance. Revenue and quota are the key metrics.
assertions: Uses a charting library or CSS bars for visualization
Handles revenue values that are strings (e.g. '285000') by parsing to numbers
Does not display NaN or undefined for missing revenue
Uses parseFloat or equivalent for numeric conversion, not Number()
Rep with empty string revenue is handled gracefully (omitted or shown as no data)
Rep with '0' revenue is shown as zero, not omitted

evals.prompt: Create a team member card grid showing who is active and who is remote.
assertions: Shows member cards with name and role
Correctly interprets string 'true'/'false' for isActive and isRemote
Does not treat string 'false' as truthy (inactive members must look visually distinct)
Member with empty string isActive is handled gracefully (treated as inactive/unknown)
Uses explicit boolean comparison (=== true or === 'true'), not bare truthiness

evals.prompt: Create a map showing CRM leads. Leads with valid coordinates should show at their location. Leads without coordinates should fall back to city/state.
assertions: Uses a mapping library for the map
Does not place markers at [0,0] for leads with empty string coordinates
Leads with empty string lat/lng use city/state fallback or are omitted
Leads with valid string coordinates (e.g. '36.1627') are parsed and placed correctly
Uses parseFloat or equivalent for coordinate parsing, not Number()
Does not use != null as the sole guard for coordinate presence
